seoTitle: How to View Multiple Inboxes at Once in Outlook 365
metaDescription: See all your Outlook 365 accounts in one place by pinning inboxes to Favorites, enabling the All Inboxes unified view, or opening a side-by-side window.
intro: Switching between separate mail profiles all day is a productivity drain, especially when time-sensitive messages land in a secondary account. This guide covers pinning multiple inboxes to your Favorites, turning on the new Outlook's unified All Inboxes view, and opening a side-by-side window for full multitasking.

steps.heading: Open the folder sidebar
steps.body: Launch Outlook and locate the folder navigation sidebar pane.

steps.heading: Pin inboxes to Favorites
steps.body: Drag separate inbox folders into the top Favorites section for quick toggling between accounts.

steps.heading: Enable All Inboxes
steps.body: In the new Outlook, turn on the integrated All Inboxes shared view to merge separate streams into one chronological feed.

steps.heading: Open a side-by-side window
steps.body: Right-click an inbox and select Open in New Window to view multiple accounts across separate monitors.

steps.heading: Build a unified search folder
steps.body: Create an All-Account Search Folder to automatically aggregate unread messages from every connected account.

steps.heading: Save your layout
steps.body: Save your customized view settings globally so your layout persists across sessions.

faq.question: Does the All Inboxes view combine every connected account?
faq.answer: Yes, it's designed to merge separate account streams into a single chronological feed within the new Outlook interface.

faq.question: Can I still tell which account a message came from in the unified view?
faq.answer: Unified views typically retain account labeling so you can identify the source even when messages are combined.

faq.question: Is opening a new window better than the unified view for multitasking?
faq.answer: Opening a separate window works well if you have multiple monitors and want dedicated, full-size views per account.

faq.question: Will my Favorites setup carry over if I switch computers?
faq.answer: Since Favorites and view settings are typically saved with your profile, they should carry over when you sign in elsewhere, though this can vary by setup.
